# Synthetic Emotional Speech Data

**Summary**: Synthetic emotional speech data is generated by another model rather than recorded from human speakers. EmoVoice uses GPT-4o-audio synthetic speech to train fine-grained emotional TTS.

**Sources**:
- Yang et al. - 2025 - EmoVoice LLM-based Emotional Text-To-Speech Model with Freestyle Text Prompting.pdf

**Last updated**: 2026-05-06

---

## Definition

Synthetic emotional speech data is speech generated by a model and used as training or evaluation material for emotion-related speech systems (source: Yang et al. - 2025 - EmoVoice LLM-based Emotional Text-To-Speech Model with Freestyle Text Prompting.pdf). [[emovoice-db]] is synthetic because its emotional speech samples are generated with GPT-4o-audio (source: Yang et al. - 2025 - EmoVoice LLM-based Emotional Text-To-Speech Model with Freestyle Text Prompting.pdf).

## Motivation

The EmoVoice paper argues that high-quality open-source emotional speech datasets are limited in size, often coarse-labeled, and may contain samples whose perceived emotion does not match the assigned label (source: Yang et al. - 2025 - EmoVoice LLM-based Emotional Text-To-Speech Model with Freestyle Text Prompting.pdf). Synthetic generation is used to create a larger, more consistently prompted dataset for [[freestyle-emotion-prompting]] (source: Yang et al. - 2025 - EmoVoice LLM-based Emotional Text-To-Speech Model with Freestyle Text Prompting.pdf).

## Evidence

The authors report that EmoVoice achieves strong performance on the EmoVoice-DB test set using only synthetic English training data (source: Yang et al. - 2025 - EmoVoice LLM-based Emotional Text-To-Speech Model with Freestyle Text Prompting.pdf). They use CosyVoice-generated synthetic data for standard TTS pretraining and GPT-4o-audio-generated synthetic data for emotional fine-tuning (source: Yang et al. - 2025 - EmoVoice LLM-based Emotional Text-To-Speech Model with Freestyle Text Prompting.pdf).

## Caution

Synthetic data can improve controllability and scale, but it may compress the target distribution into the teacher model's style and errors (source: Yang et al. - 2025 - EmoVoice LLM-based Emotional Text-To-Speech Model with Freestyle Text Prompting.pdf). For research on [[speaker-emotion-tradeoff]], synthetic TTS data does not directly answer whether a voice-conversion model preserves emotion while changing speaker identity.
